question: Who is the project PI on the Lower Sammish River PFAS Monitoring Study?
reference_answer: Dr. Henrike Lentz, Associate Professor, Department of Environmental Chemistry.

question: What is the parent sample identifier for the water sample collected at site SR-7?
What instrument was used for analysis on May 1, 2026?
reference_answer: LC-MS/MS Q-Exactive instrument INST-04.

question: What is the run number for the May 1 analysis?
Who collected the field sample on April 28, 2026?
reference_answer: Tara Mendelssohn, graduate student on the LSR-PFAS team.

question: What analytical method was requested for this sample?
reference_answer: EPA Method 1633 by LC-MS/MS, for PFOA, PFOS, and GenX.

question: Who is the Lab Director who issued the calibration curve reprocessing memo?
reference_answer: Dr. Margaret Hsiao.

question: What is the cap lot number flagged in the vial cap manufacturer advisory?
Which source records the freezer temperature excursion that began at 02:14 on April 29?
reference_answer: The freezer F-3 telemetry log from HOBO U30 logger FZ-LOG-03.

question: Which document records the manufacturer's notification regarding the LC vial cap lot?
reference_answer: The Acme Vial Co. quality-assurance email received May 5, 2026 at 08:55 PDT.

question: According to which source did Tu enter the prep room at 13:42 on April 30?
reference_answer: The badge access log (HID prox card) for the prep room and freezer alcove.

question: Which system records the chain-of-custody log entries?
reference_answer: LabWare LIMS v.7.4 (the Trace Analytical Facility's LIMS).

question: Which document recorded the calibration reprocessing that changed the PFOA value?
reference_answer: The calibration curve reprocessing memo filed by Hsiao on May 5, 2026 at 11:14 PDT.

question: Whose lab notebook entry recorded the manual freezer check at 11:30 on May 4?
reference_answer: Sam Okeke's lab notebook entry on May 4, 2026 at 11:30 PDT.

question: As of packet time, what is the reported value of PFOA in sample LS-PFAS-2026-0428-SR7-A?
reference_answer: 168 ng/L, per the corrected report (version 1.1) issued May 5, 2026.

question: What was the initial reported PFOA value before the erratum?
reference_answer: 142 ng/L, per the initial report (version 1.0) issued May 2, 2026.

question: Per SOP TAF-SOP-RPT-02 §5.3, which report version is the report of record after an erratum?
reference_answer: The corrected report. Per TAF-SOP-RPT-02 §5.3, when an erratum is issued, the corrected report is the report of record; prior versions are retained for audit but are not the report of record.

question: Did the erratum change the reported PFOS value? If so, to what?
reference_answer: No. PFOS (89 ng/L) and GenX (12 ng/L) were unchanged because their respective internal standards were unaffected by the COA revision.

question: Was the May 1 analysis of aliquot A affected by the May 4–5 freezer failure?
reference_answer: No. Aliquot A was analyzed on May 1, 2026, before the May 4–5 freezer failure; its results are unaffected by that excursion.

question: As of packet time, where is aliquot B located?
reference_answer: Aliquot B remains in freezer F-3 as a backup, per the LIMS chain-of-custody log.

question: As of May 4 at 11:30, what was freezer F-3's temperature according to the manual check, and who performed it?
reference_answer: -20 °C, no anomalies; manual check performed by Sam Okeke.

question: What was freezer F-3's temperature behavior between May 4 19:48 and May 5 06:32, and what was the cause?
reference_answer: Sustained excursion to a maximum of -8 °C, duration 10 hours 44 minutes, caused by compressor failure. Maintenance ticket FAC-2026-0428 opened May 5 at 06:35; the freezer returned to specification at 07:48 PDT on May 5.

question: As of packet time, has the contamination review been completed?
reference_answer: No. The contamination review is scheduled for May 8, 2026 at 10:00 PDT and has not been held.

question: As of May 1 at 09:14, what was the operational status of instrument INST-04 per the run log?
reference_answer: Operational. System suitability QC was PASS for the run started at 09:14 PDT on May 1.

question: Per EPA Method 1633, what is the acceptable range for internal standard recovery, and was the May 1 PFOA-13C8 recovery within range?
reference_answer: 50–150% per EPA Method 1633. The May 1 PFOA-13C8 recovery of 87% was within range.

question: According to the packet, who has authority to issue the contamination determination?
reference_answer: The Lab Director, Dr. Margaret Hsiao.

question: Per the chain-of-custody log convention, who must sign at sample transfer between custody points?
reference_answer: Each transferring party must sign, and a co-witness signature is required at the split (per the LIMS log entries showing both signer and co-witness).

question: What is the freezer F-3 temperature specification?
Per laboratory SOP TAF-SOP-RPT-02 §5.3, which report version governs after an erratum has been issued?
reference_answer: The corrected report (version 1.1). Prior versions are retained for audit but are not the report of record.

question: How many distinct freezer F-3 temperature excursions are recorded in the packet between April 28 and May 5?
reference_answer: Three. April 29 02:14–02:32 (max -16 °C, 18 minutes, scheduled auto-defrost); May 1 03:22–03:49 (max -14 °C, 27 minutes, compressor cycling); and May 4 19:48 – May 5 06:32 (max -8 °C, 10 hours 44 minutes, compressor failure).

question: How many cap lots were in active use in the lab during April 25 – May 1, 2026?
reference_answer: Two: LCV-2026-031 (subject to the advisory) and LCV-2026-029 (no advisory).

question: How many distinct named personnel are recorded in the chain-of-custody log for sample LS-PFAS-2026-0428-SR7-A?
reference_answer: Three distinct named personnel: Tara Mendelssohn (collector), Sam Okeke (intake and co-witness), and Brendan Tu (split and run).

question: List the dates of all procedural anchors for this sample from collection through erratum issuance.
reference_answer: Six dates: April 28 (collection), April 29 (intake), April 30 (split), May 1 (run), May 2 (initial report v1.0), and May 5 (calibration reprocessing memo and erratum v1.1).

question: Has it been determined whether vial cap lot LCV-2026-031 was used for aliquot A?
reference_answer: No, not determined. The lab cannot determine which cap lot was used; supplier shipment-tracing data has been requested but not received.

question: Has Dr. Hsiao issued the formal contamination determination?
reference_answer: No. The contamination review is scheduled for May 8, 2026 at 10:00 PDT; no determination has been issued.

question: Is the integrity of aliquot B confirmed as of packet time?
reference_answer: No. The May 4–5 freezer failure put aliquot B outside the -20 °C ± 1 °C specification; integrity is pending Hsiao's documented determination.

question: Did Tu's bench notebook specify which cap lot was used for the April 30 split?
reference_answer: No. The notebook explicitly states Tu did not record the specific cap lot.

question: Tu's notebook records the split as completed at 13:45, but the freezer logger records a door event at 14:00. Does this constitute a conflict, and how is it resolved?
reference_answer: Not a conflict. Tu's notebook records the bench split completion at 13:45, while the freezer door event at 14:00 corresponds to the subsequent return of aliquot B to freezer F-3 (LIMS records aliquot B return at 14:02). The two events describe different actions in the same workflow.

question: The vial cap memo says lot LCV-2026-031 may contribute PFAS background; the May 1 run's internal standard recovery was 87% (within method acceptance). Does this resolve the contamination question?
reference_answer: No. The 87% internal standard recovery indicates the run met EPA Method 1633 system suitability for that internal standard, but it does not address the possibility of cap-lot background contribution to PFAS signal. The contamination question remains pending Hsiao's May 8 determination.

question: The initial report on May 2 stated PFOA at 142 ng/L; the erratum on May 5 states 168 ng/L. Which value is currently authoritative for the watershed monitoring study?
reference_answer: 168 ng/L per the corrected report (version 1.1) issued May 5, 2026. The 142 ng/L value from the initial report (version 1.0) is superseded.
